assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines available version as one that's currently available for manual upgrades that users can choose to upgrade to

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — typically the same as auto-upgrade target but can differ especially during new minor version introduction (separate promotion stage)
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines default as the version new clusters will use when created and notes it can differ from auto-upgrade target, though it doesn't specifically mention the separate promotion stage concept

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically — this is cluster-specific based on policies
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines auto-upgrade target as the version clusters will be upgraded to automatically and mentions it's based on upgrade policies

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response explains these can be different and provides context about gradual rollouts and safe upgrade paths, though doesn't explicitly mention minor version rollouts specifically

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions) and is cluster-specific — different clusters on the same channel can have different targets
assertions.reason: Response mentions upgrade policies but doesn't specifically discuss maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions, or that different clusters on the same channel can have different targets

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the concept of 'no minor' exclusions affecting auto-upgrade targets

assertions.expectation: Recommends using gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info to check the actual auto-upgrade status, target versions, and EoS timestamps
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the specific gcloud command or provide actionable steps for checking upgrade information

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response does not reference GKE release schedule, release notes, or other documentation resources for tracking versions
